data.title: Canada Ends Rebates on Tesla (TSLA) Vehicles
data.description:
British Columbia, Canada’s west coast province, is ending subsidies and rebates that previously applied to Tesla (TSLA) electric vehicles and their charging stations.

The government in B.C., one of Canada's most populous and pro-environment provinces, has announced that Tesla vehicles are no longer eligible for rebates through programs aimed at incentivizing clean energy products.

The move, according to the government, is being made “to preference Canadian goods in our rebate programs going forward and to exclude, where practicable, U.S. produced goods.” The exclusion targets billionaire Elon Musk's main company amid a trade war prompted by U.S. President Donald Trump imposing tariffs on Canadian imports.

Canadian Ties

Musk, a close Trump ally, has used his social media platform to make statements disparaging outgoing Prime Minister Justin Trudeau and to say that “Canada is not a real country.” The irony is that Musk himself is a Canadian citizen. Musk's mother, Maye Haldeman, is from Saskatchewan and Musk spent a lot of time in Canada growing up. He also attended Queen's University in Kingston, Ontario.

As a teenager, Musk used his Canadian citizenship to avoid South Africa’s mandatory military service. Despite his disparaging remarks, Musk has maintained his Canadian citizenship and passport after relocating to the U.S. as an adult. But now, Canada appears to have soured on Musk and his comments about the country.

A petition to revoke Musk's Canadian citizenship on the grounds that he has “engaged in activities that go against the national interest of Canada” and “attacked Canadian sovereignty” has amassed hundreds of thousands of signatures.

Tesla Chargers

B.C. Energy Minister Adrian Dix said taxpayer dollars should no longer subsidize Tesla vehicles. The British Columbia subsidies and rebates mostly applied to Tesla chargers, batteries and inverters. The rebates and subsidies previously covered as much as 50% of the cost of purchasing and installing a Tesla home charger, according to the provincial government.

That coverage previously saved Tesla vehicle owners as much as $10,000 per home charger. Anyone who was approved for a rebate before March 12 will still be able to claim it, according to the B.C. government. TSLA stock has declined 40% so far this year.

Is TSLA Stock a Buy?

Tesla stock currently has a consensus Hold rating among 37 Wall Street analysts. That rating is based on 12 Buy, 13 Hold, and 12 Sell recommendations issued in the last three months. The average TSLA price target of $330.90 implies 37.49% upside from current levels.

data.author: Joel Baglole

data.title: Gold Shatters Records as Bitcoin Stumbles amid Trump-Era Uncertainty
data.description:
Gold (CM:XAUUSD) is proving why it's been the go-to safe-haven asset for centuries. The precious metal just crossed the $3,000 per ounce mark for the first time, fueled by market uncertainty, strong ETF inflows, and concerns over U.S. tariffs under President Donald Trump, according to CoinDesk. Gold is up over 15% this year, while Bitcoin has lost 24% since hitting its January high of over $109,000.

This divergence shows us a key reality: while Bitcoin is often called “digital gold,” the real stuff still holds the crown when fear grips the market. As Yardeni Research strategist Eric Wallerstein put it, "Gold is a safe haven… Bitcoin, on the other hand, is a super speculative asset."

The numbers tell the story. Over the past 30 days, gold ETFs have raked in $10 billion, while Bitcoin ETFs have lost $5 billion, according to ByTree founder Charlie Morris. He believes this trend will eventually reverse—just as it always has—but for now, investors are choosing stability over risk.

Trump-Era Policy Uncertainty Fuels Gold's Rise

Trump's unpredictable governing style is also playing a role. His back-and-forth stance on tariffs, ongoing geopolitical tensions, and efforts to reset U.S.-Russia relations have investors uneasy. WisdomTree analyst Nitesh Shah pointed out that "previous alliances are being redrawn," leading to increased demand for gold as a hedge.

Bitcoin, on the other hand, soared after Trump's election win amid hopes of favorable crypto regulation. But reality has set in—Bitcoin’s price has dipped, and enthusiasm around Trump's $TRUMP meme coin hasn’t helped investor confidence.

At the time of writing, Bitcoin is still hovering around $83,200. For now, it’s behaving more like a high-risk tech stock than a reliable store of value. Gold, meanwhile, is doing what it does best—offering a safe escape when uncertainty takes over. Prices are now hovering at $2,998 per ounce.

Questions or Comments about the article? Write to editor@tipranks.com
data.author: Annika Masrani

data.title: Can Nvidia's Stock Keep Climbing as Foxconn Bets Big on AI?
data.description:
Nvidia (NVDA) is on the move again. Shares ticked up 1.7% to $117.58 in premarket trading on Friday, and this time, it's not just AI hype—it's real numbers. Foxconn, the world's biggest electronics manufacturer and a key Nvidia supplier, just laid out a bold forecast. The company expects its AI server revenue to blast past 1 trillion New Taiwanese dollars ($30 billion) in 2025, according to Reuters. That's the kind of number that makes Wall Street sit up and pay attention.

Foxconn's earnings weren't perfect—profits slid in Q4—but that AI revenue projection overshadowed the dip. The takeaway? AI infrastructure demand isn't slowing down anytime soon, and Nvidia's chips are at the heart of it.

Tariffs Threaten Nvidia's AI Momentum

But before Nvidia investors get too comfortable, there's a wildcard in the mix: tariffs. U.S. President Donald Trump's latest trade policies have made markets volatile. This has raised concerns about inflation and economic slowdowns. Even so, Foxconn's bullish outlook suggests that when it comes to AI, companies are willing to keep spending.

Is Nvidia a Buy or Hold?

Over on Wall Street, analysts aren't just optimistic—they're all in. Nvidia holds a Strong Buy consensus rating, with 39 analysts calling it a Buy and only three sitting on the fence with a Hold. No one's recommending a Sell. On top of that, the average NVDA price target sits at $177.41 per share, suggesting a potential 53.5% upside. If these predictions hold, Nvidia's rally might still have plenty of room to run.

See more NVDA analyst ratings

data.author: Annika Masrani

data.title: Tesla's Dream Turns to Chaos as Sales Drop and Protests Erupt
data.description:
Tesla (TSLA) is no longer the invincible giant some investors once believed it to be. Its dream is now turning to chaos as sales decline, protests erupt, and doubts grow over its future. While it remains part of the so-called “Magnificent Seven,” its market performance tells a different story. Unlike its tech-heavy peers—Apple (AAPL), Microsoft (MSFT), and Nvidia (NVDA)—Tesla is struggling with a sharp drop in profitability, wild stock swings, and mounting skepticism over its future, according to Barron's.

The company's operating margin in 2024 was just 7%, far below the 37% average of its Magnificent Seven peers. Tesla also trades at a staggering 85 times earnings, a valuation that far outstrips its competition. Moreover, Wall Street price targets range from $25 to $550 per share. This has left investors guessing where Tesla truly stands.

Global Backlash and Falling Sales Create Investor Panic

Beyond the numbers, Tesla faces growing backlash worldwide. Sales in the U.S., Europe, and China dropped 20% in January, while global protests erupted outside Tesla stores. Some customers are boycotting purchases, reselling their Teslas, and even damaging charging stations.

According to ABC News, authorities in Massachusetts are investigating a series of fires that caused severe damage to seven Tesla charging stations on March 3. The Littleton Police Department confirmed that officials believe the fires were “deliberately” set.

J.P. Morgan (JPM) analyst Ryan Brinkman summed it up bluntly: “We struggle to think of anything analogous in the history of the automotive industry, in which a brand has lost so much value so quickly.”

Musk Holds the Key to Tesla's Future

Much of Tesla's fate remains tied to Elon Musk. While his influence has fueled both meteoric rises and dramatic crashes, investors are now looking for stability. Wedbush analyst Daniel Ives suggested that if Musk can refocus on Tesla, deliver a lower-cost vehicle, and launch the long-promised robo-taxi service, the company could regain credibility.

For now, Tesla is built on dreams—and whether those dreams survive depends on Musk himself.

Is Tesla a Buy, Sell, or Hold?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Hold consensus rating on TSLA stock based on 12 Buys, 13 Holds, and 12 Sells assigned in the past three months. Furthermore, the average TSLA price target of $331.07 per share implies 37.6% upside potential.

See more TSLA analyst ratings

data.author: Annika Masrani

data.title: XRP Price Poised for 46% Surge as SEC Case Moves Toward Resolution
data.description:
XRP (XRP-USD) is on the verge of a major breakout. The token has been consolidating inside a descending triangle since hitting a seven-year high of $3.40 in January. But now, it's pushing against a critical resistance level at $2.30, and if it breaks through, a surge to $3.40 could be in play—a potential 46% gain, according to Cointelegraph. With Ripple securing a key Dubai license and its SEC case nearing resolution, these fundamental catalysts could further fuel the rally.

Crypto analyst CrediBull Crypto pointed out that XRP's recent dip below $2.00 provided an ideal entry point for buyers. Now, as the price forms higher lows, momentum is building. If XRP clears the $3.00 psychological level, the path toward its multi-year high at $3.40 becomes a lot clearer.

Ripple's Dubai License and SEC Case Fuel Optimism

Beyond technicals, Ripple has secured its first-ever regulatory approval in the Middle East. The Dubai Financial Services Authority has greenlit Ripple's crypto payment service. This approval grants Ripple access to the U.A.E.'s $40 billion remittance market.

Following the announcement, XRP's price jumped 6% to $2.34. Crypto commentator Vincent van Code called the approval a "game-changer," saying it positions Ripple as a leader in regulated crypto payments.

Meanwhile, Ripple's battle with the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) could finally be nearing an end. Fox Business reporter Eleanor Terret revealed that both parties might drop their appeals, with sources suggesting the SEC's new leadership is shifting away from aggressive crypto enforcement.

If the case officially wraps up, it could remove a major cloud hanging over XRP—just in time for a breakout. At the time of writing, XRP is sitting at $2.3147.

data.author: Annika Masrani

data.title: Stock Market News Today, 3/14/25 – Futures Rebound After Thursday's Slump
data.description:
U.S. stock futures moved higher on early Friday following yesterday's slump due to the threat of new tariffs by President Donald Trump. Futures on the Nasdaq 100 (NDX), the Dow Jones Industrial Average (DJIA), and the S&P 500 (SPX) were up 0.87%, 0.43%, and 0.62%, respectively, at 4:30 a.m. EST, March 14.

On Thursday, the S&P 500 and the Nasdaq Composite declined 1.39% and 1.96%, respectively, while the Dow Jones Industrial Average fell 1.3% amid concerns over tariffs. With yesterday's decline, the S&P 500 is down more than 10% from its last month's record levels, bringing the benchmark into correction territory.

On his Truth Social platform, Trump threatened 200% tariffs on all alcoholic products imported from countries in the European Union in retaliation to the bloc's 50% tariff on whisky. The trade wars sparked by tariffs overshadowed the softer-than-anticipated inflation reading for February.

Stocks trending in Thursday's after-hours included Ulta Beauty (ULTA) and DocuSign (DOCU). Ulta gained on better-than-expected fourth-quarter results. Electronics signature software company DocuSign also impressed investors with upbeat Q4 results.

Coming to key economic releases, investors await Consumer Sentiment data as it will reflect the impact of ongoing challenges and tariffs on consumers' confidence.

On the earnings front, traders’ focus will be on the fourth-quarter results of Chinese electric vehicle maker Li Auto (LI). Analysts’ expectations are high as the company delivered strong delivery numbers despite ongoing challenges.

Meanwhile, the U.S. 10-year treasury yield was up, floating near 4.285%. Simultaneously, WTI crude oil futures are trending higher, hovering near $67.31 per barrel as of the last check.

Elsewhere, European indices opened higher today even as tariffs and retaliatory measures continue to be in focus.  

Asia-Pacific Markets Ended Higher on Friday

Asia-Pacific indices closed higher on Friday despite the fear around tariffs. Hong Kong's Hang Seng Index was up 2.1%.

Further, China's Shanghai Composite and Shenzhen Component indices rose 1.81% and 2.3%, respectively.

Also, Japan's Nikkei index closed higher by 0.72% while the Topix index increased 0.65%.

data.title: Intel's (INTC) New CEO Brings Hope, but Analysts Are Still Cautious
data.description:
Intel's (INTC) appointment of Lip-Bu Tan as its new Chief Executive Officer has sparked optimism in the market. Following the announcement, Intel's stock surged 14.6% yesterday, signaling investor confidence in Tan's leadership as a potential fresh start for the chip giant. However, despite the market's excitement, most analysts remain hesitant to recommend the stock.

Analysts Welcome the Move but Stay on the Fence

While Tan's appointment has created a positive buzz, analysts are not rushing to change their stance to Buy. Bernstein analyst Stacy Rasgon praised Tan's realistic approach but warned that fixing Intel's problems will take time. Other analysts share similar concerns and have stuck to their Neutral ratings. Out of 32 analysts tracking Intel on TipRanks, 27 say hold.

Analyst Stacy Rasgon highlighted several challenges that Intel needs to overcome to win investors' trust again. He pointed out that the company lacks a customer service mindset, which is “increasingly important to their goals but has been lacking.” He warned that the new CEO has “a big job in front of him and a lot of wood to chop.” He also suggested that if Tan cannot drive a turnaround, “it was probably unfixable,” emphasizing the scale of Intel's internal challenges. Also, he noted Intel's struggles within its broader ecosystem, including foundries and design partners, which will require major changes to resolve.

With Intel set to announce its first-quarter 2025 results next month, all eyes will be on Tan's strategy and any new changes he plans to implement. 

Is INTC Stock a Buy?

The stock of Intel has a consensus Hold rating among 32 Wall Street analysts. That rating is based on one Buy, 27 Hold, and four Sell recommendations issued in the last three months. The average INTC price target of $23 implies 2.95% downside from current levels.

See more INTC analyst ratings

data.title: Comcast-owned NBC Signs Massive $3B Deal to Air Olympic Games Until 2036
data.description:
Communications titan Comcast (CMCSA)-owned NBC just locked in a $3 billion deal to keep broadcasting the Olympic Games through 2036. This new agreement with the International Olympic Committee (IOC) means NBC will air the 2034 Winter Olympics, likely in Salt Lake City, and the 2036 Summer Olympics, with the host city still up in the air.

The deal extends NBC's long-standing relationship with the Olympics, which began in 1964. The network has aired every Summer Olympics since 1988 and every Winter Olympics since 2002. With this extension, NBC remains the go-to platform for American viewers to experience the world’s biggest sporting event.

NBC Expands Its Digital Push for Future Olympics

NBC isn't just sticking to regular TV broadcasts. The network is working with the IOC to boost digital ads and improve how the Games are shown using Comcast's tech.

During the Paris Olympics last year, NBC streamed every event live on its Peacock platform while running highlight shows. The coverage drew an average of 67 million viewers a day across NBC's platforms and brought in $1.2 billion in ad revenue.

With the 2028 Olympics set for Los Angeles, analysts predict even bigger audiences as the Games will be held in the U.S. for the first time since the 2002 Winter Olympics in Salt Lake City.

Live Sports Still Rule the Game

Despite the rise of streaming services, live sports continue to hold their value in the U.S. market. Just last year, the National Basketball Association (NBA) secured a massive 11-year, $76 billion deal.

However, not every streaming effort has been successful. At the start of this year, NBC's rivals, Walt Disney (DIS), Fox (FOX), and Warner Bros. Discovery (WBD), scrapped plans for a sports-focused streaming service called Venu. The platform aimed to bundle major events like the NFL, NBA, and FIFA World Cup to capture younger viewers who avoid traditional cable.

Is Comcast Stock a Good Buy Right Now?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Moderate Buy consensus rating on CMCSA stock based on 10 Buys and nine Holds assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. After a 16.39% loss in its share price over the past year, the average CMCSA price target of $41.58 per share implies 19.83% upside potential.

See more CMCSA analyst ratings

data.title: DOCU Earnings: DocuSign Stock Jumps 10% as Financial Results Beat Wall Street Targets
data.description:
The stock of DocuSign (DOCU) is up 10% after the software company issued financial results that beat Wall Street targets on the top and bottom lines.

The San Francisco-based company announced fourth-quarter 2024 earnings per share (EPS) of $0.86, which was ahead of the $0.85 expected among analysts who track the company’s progress. Revenue in the quarter came in at $776.3 million, which also topped consensus estimates of $760.99 million.

The company, which is known for its electronic signature software, provided forward guidance that fell short of expectations. Management said they expect revenue in the current quarter of $745 million to $749 million. That was below analysts’ consensus forecast of $755.70 million.

Billings and Stock Buybacks

DocuSign’s billings increased to $923.2 million in the latest quarter, exceeding management’s forecast and marking a year-over-year increase of 10%. The company said this result reflects successful international growth and product diversification. Subscription revenue reached $757.8 million, a 9% increase from last year’s $695.7 million.

Despite the strong overall growth, DocuSign’s professional services unit saw a slight negative margin impact, pointing to ongoing market challenges. The company significantly increased its stock repurchases in recent months. For all of last year, DocuSign bought back $683.5 million of company stock, up from $145.5 million in 2023.

Full-Year Outlook

For all of this year, DocuSign anticipates revenue of $3.13 billion to $3.14 billion. That too was a little below analysts’ consensus target of $3.15 billion. Investors’ didn’t appear too concerned with the company’s latest guidance, bidding up the share price after hours.

DocuSign looks to have started turning around its business after a slump coming out of the Covid-19 pandemic. Management has said they are focused on expanding the company’s product portfolio and leveraging innovation to drive current and future growth.

DOCU stock has declined 17% so far in 2025.

Is DOCU Stock a Buy?

The stock of DocuSign has a consensus Moderate Buy rating among six Wall Street analysts. That rating is based on three Buy and three Hold recommendations assigned in the last three months. The average DOCU price target of $103.40 implies 38.42% upside from current levels. These ratings are likely to change after the company’s latest financial results.

Read more analyst ratings on DOCU stock

data.title: Stock Market News Today, 3/13/25 – Futures Slide amid New Tariff Threats
data.description:
Last Updated: 4:36 PM EST

Stock indices finished today's trading session in the red after President Trump threatened to impose a 200% tariff on all EU-imported alcoholic products. As a result, the Nasdaq 100 (NDX), the S&P 500 (SPX), and the Dow Jones Industrial Average (DJIA) fell 1.89%, 1.39%, and 1.3%, respectively.

However, the U.S. economy did receive some good news on Thursday. First off, the Producer Price Index (PPI) fell 0.1% in February compared to expectations of a 0.3% increase. This slowdown in wholesale inflation follows a cooler-than-expected Consumer Price Index (CPI) report earlier this month. The PPI data suggests that inflation pressures may be easing and giving the Federal Reserve policymakers some breathing room.

Separately, the Labor Department reported a drop in initial jobless claims for the week ended March 8. Claims fell by 2,000 to 220,000, beating expectations of 226,000. This suggests that the labor market remains strong despite concerns about the impact of tariffs and a potential recession. Continuing claims also fell, dropping to 1.87 million, below the expected 1.9 million.

Interestingly, today’s suggest that the economy is stable and the job market is strong. However, the ongoing tariff drama and recession fears continue to weigh on financial markets. As a result, the Federal Reserve will continue to closely watch these metrics in order to decide how it will react at the next meeting.

First Published: 5:06 AM EST

U.S. stock futures fell early Thursday, after yesterday's positive session on a cooler-than-expected inflation report. Futures on the Nasdaq 100 (NDX), the Dow Jones Industrial Average (DJIA), and the S&P 500 (SPX) were down 0.83%, 0.41%, and 0.58%, respectively, at 4:52 a.m. EST, March 13.

Wednesday’s regular trading session witnessed a strong rebound, with the S&P 500 and the Nasdaq Composite up 0.49% and 1.22%, respectively, driven by a surge in Nvidia (NVDA) and Palantir Technologies (PLTR). However, the Dow Jones declined 0.2%.

The release of February’s consumer price index came in slightly softer than expected, and provided relief to investors concerned about inflation. However, concerns remain about the potential impact of President Trump’s tariffs on steel and aluminum, as well as retaliatory tariffs from Canada.  

In after-market action, Intel (INTC) surged about 10% on news of a new CEO appointment, while Adobe (ADBE) slipped roughly 4% due to weaker-than-expected earnings forecasts.

Looking ahead, investors are awaiting key economic reports such as the Weekly Jobless Claims for the week ended March 8 and the February Producer Price Index. These numbers are important as they may guide the Federal Reserve's next moves on interest rates.

On the earnings front, several companies such as Dollar General (DG), DocuSign (DOCU), D-Wave Quantum (QBTS), Blink Charging (BLNK), and Ulta Beauty (ULTA) are set to release their quarterly numbers today.

Meanwhile, the U.S. 10-year treasury yield was down, floating near 4.312%. Simultaneously, WTI crude oil futures are trending higher, hovering near $67.82 per barrel as of the last check.

Elsewhere, European indices opened lower today as investors are worried over disruptions in global trade caused by Trump’s tariff policies.  

Asia-Pacific Markets Ended Lower on Thursday

Most of the Asia-Pacific indices were in the red today. Relief over cooler U.S. consumer inflation data was offset by concerns about a global trade war as Trump’s steel and aluminum tariffs took effect.

At the same time, Hong Kong's Hang Seng Index was down 0.58%. Further, China's Shanghai Composite and Shenzhen Component indices declined 0.39% and 0.99%, respectively. Also, Japan's Nikkei index fell 0.08%, while the Topix index was up 0.13%.

Interested in more economic insights? Tune in to our LIVE webinar.

Disclosure
